seoTitle: Starrystone Tech Products - Power Semiconductor & LED Driver Solutions
seoDescription: Explore Starrystone Tech's comprehensive product portfolio including AC-DC controllers, DC-DC converters, LED drivers, and gate drivers. LiTong is your authorized distributor.

faqs.question: What product categories does Starrystone Tech offer?
faqs.answer: Starrystone Tech offers four main product categories: AC-DC Controllers including PWM controllers and synchronous rectifiers for power supplies; DC-DC Converters including buck, boost, and buck-boost converters for voltage conversion; LED Drivers for general lighting and display applications; and Gate Drivers for controlling power MOSFETs and IGBTs. Each category includes multiple product series targeting different application requirements and performance levels.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories to find the right solution for your application. Contact our FAE team for selection guidance.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Starrystone Tech product for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Starrystone Tech product involves several steps. First, identify your application requirements including input voltage range, output voltage/current, and power level. Second, determine the converter topology needed (flyback, buck, boost, etc.). Third, consider efficiency requirements and protection features needed. Fourth, evaluate package options and thermal requirements. Fifth, check for specific features like synchronous rectification or dimming control. Starrystone Tech provides selection guides and our FAE team can provide personalized recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our selection guides or contact our FAE team for personalized product recommendations based on your application requirements.

faqs.question: What are the key differences between Starrystone Tech's AC-DC controller series?
faqs.answer: Starrystone Tech's AC-DC controller series differ in power level, topology support, and features. Some series focus on low-power applications (under 30W) with high integration and minimal external components. Others target medium to high power (up to 100W+) with advanced features like active PFC and synchronous rectification. The STR-A series offers cost-effective solutions for standard adapters, while STR-B series provides higher performance with advanced protection features. Understanding these differences helps select the optimal solution for your specific power supply design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Compare power levels and features to select the right controller. Contact us for detailed comparison and recommendations.

faqs.question: Which Starrystone Tech products are suitable for LED lighting applications?
faqs.answer: Starrystone Tech offers several product series specifically designed for LED lighting applications. The STR-L series LED drivers provide constant current output with various current levels for different LED configurations. These drivers support analog and PWM dimming, making them suitable for both general lighting and smart lighting applications. For high-power LED applications, the STR-H series offers higher current capability and better thermal performance. The products support various topologies including buck, boost, and buck-boost for different LED string configurations.
faqs.decisionGuide: For LED lighting designs, consider the STR-L series for standard applications or STR-H series for high-power requirements. Request reference designs from our team.

faqs.question: What DC-DC converter topologies does Starrystone Tech support?
faqs.answer: Starrystone Tech provides DC-DC converters supporting multiple topologies. Buck (step-down) converters are available for applications where input voltage is higher than output. Boost (step-up) converters suit applications requiring higher output voltage. Buck-boost converters handle both step-up and step-down operation, ideal for battery-powered applications where input voltage varies. The product portfolio covers various current and voltage ranges to meet diverse application requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select topology based on your input/output voltage relationship. Contact us for topology selection guidance.

categories.name: AC-DC Controllers
categories.description: High-efficiency PWM controllers and synchronous rectifiers for switching power supplies, adapters, and chargers.
categories.longDescription: Starrystone Tech's AC-DC controller portfolio provides comprehensive solutions for switching power supply applications. The product range includes PWM controllers for various topologies (flyback, forward, etc.) and synchronous rectifier controllers for improved efficiency. These controllers feature high integration, reducing external component count while providing advanced protection functions. With support for power levels from a few watts to over 100W, Starrystone Tech AC-DC controllers suit applications ranging from mobile chargers to industrial power supplies. The products are designed to meet efficiency and safety standards including DoE Level VI and CoC Tier 2. LiTong is your authorized distributor providing selection guidance and technical support for Starrystone Tech products.

series.name: STR-A Series
series.description: Cost-effective PWM controllers for standard power adapters

series.name: STR-SR Series
series.description: Synchronous rectifier controllers for high-efficiency designs

selectionGuide.title: How to Select AC-DC Controllers
selectionGuide.description: Consider power level, topology, efficiency requirements, and protection features

faqs.question: What is the difference between STR-A and STR-SR series?
faqs.answer: The STR-A series includes PWM controllers for primary-side power conversion, providing the main control function in flyback and forward converters. The STR-SR series consists of synchronous rectifier controllers for secondary-side operation, replacing diode rectifiers with MOSFETs to improve efficiency. STR-A controllers are used alone for basic designs or combined with STR-SR for high-efficiency applications. Both series work together to create complete high-efficiency power supply solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use STR-A for primary-side control. Add STR-SR for synchronous rectification to improve efficiency. Contact us for complete solution recommendations.

faqs.question: What protection features are integrated in Starrystone Tech controllers?
faqs.answer: Starrystone Tech AC-DC controllers integrate comprehensive protection features including over-voltage protection (OVP), over-current protection (OCP), over-load protection (OLP), short-circuit protection (SCP), and over-temperature protection (OTP). These protections ensure safe operation under abnormal conditions and protect both the power supply and connected devices. The controllers also feature soft-start functionality to limit inrush current during startup.
faqs.decisionGuide: The integrated protections simplify design and improve reliability. Review the datasheet for specific protection thresholds and characteristics.

faqs.question: How do I achieve high efficiency with Starrystone Tech controllers?
faqs.answer: High efficiency can be achieved through several design choices: Use synchronous rectification (STR-SR series) instead of diode rectifiers to reduce conduction losses; Optimize transformer design for minimal losses; Select low-RDS(on) MOSFETs for primary and secondary switches; Implement proper snubber circuits to reduce switching losses; Use appropriate inductor and capacitor values; and Design for proper thermal management. Starrystone Tech reference designs demonstrate high-efficiency implementations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow reference designs for high-efficiency implementations. Contact our FAE team for optimization guidance.

faqs.question: What is the maximum power supported by Starrystone Tech controllers?
faqs.answer: Starrystone Tech AC-DC controllers support a wide power range depending on the specific model. Low-power controllers (STR-A1xx series) are suitable for applications up to 30W, such as mobile chargers. Medium-power controllers (STR-A3xx series) support up to 65W for laptop adapters. High-power controllers (STR-A5xx series) can handle 100W and above for high-power applications. The actual maximum power depends on the external power stage design and thermal management.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select controller based on your power requirements. Contact us for specific model recommendations.

faqs.question: Do Starrystone Tech controllers support active PFC?
faqs.answer: Some Starrystone Tech controller models include active Power Factor Correction (PFC) functionality for applications requiring high power factor and low harmonic distortion. These controllers are suitable for designs above 75W where PFC is typically required by regulations. The integrated PFC simplifies design by combining PFC and PWM control in a single IC. For applications not requiring PFC, standard controllers without PFC are available at lower cost.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select PFC-enabled controllers for applications above 75W or where power factor requirements apply. Contact us for PFC controller options.

products.name: High-Efficiency PWM Controller
products.shortDescription: High-performance PWM controller for flyback converters with integrated 650V MOSFET and advanced protection
descriptionParagraphs: The STR-A6069H is a high-efficiency PWM controller designed for flyback converter applications.
It integrates a 650V power MOSFET and comprehensive protection features, minimizing external component count.
The controller supports quasi-resonant operation for improved efficiency and reduced EMI.

faeReview.author: James Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Solutions
faeReview.content: The STR-A6069H is an excellent choice for compact power adapter designs up to 30W. The integrated MOSFET significantly reduces PCB space and component count. I particularly appreciate the quasi-resonant operation which provides excellent efficiency while keeping EMI low. The built-in protections are comprehensive and reliable. In my experience supporting numerous adapter designs, this controller consistently delivers stable performance. The low standby power easily meets modern efficiency standards. For designers creating compact chargers or adapters, this IC provides a cost-effective, high-performance solution.
faeReview.highlight: Integrated MOSFET with quasi-resonant operation for high efficiency

alternativeParts.comparison: STR-A6069H=><LNK3202: Output current 4A > 2A, suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: STR-A6069H offers higher current capability and power rating at competitive cost
alternativeParts.useCase: STR-A6069H for higher power requirements; LNK3202 for lower power applications

alternativeParts.comparison: STR-A6069H=><iW1702: Output current 4A > 2A (+100%), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: STR-A6069H provides higher power capability with similar features
alternativeParts.useCase: STR-A6069H for higher power designs; iW1702 for standard applications

companionParts.description: Synchronous rectifier controller for improved efficiency
Secondary side feedback IC for precise regulation
EE16 or EE19 transformer for flyback converter
Low ESR electrolytic capacitors for output filtering

faqs.question: What is the maximum output power of STR-A6069H?
faqs.answer: The STR-A6069H supports up to 30W output power in typical flyback applications. The actual maximum power depends on the input voltage range, thermal design, and external component selection. For universal input (85-265VAC) applications, derating may be necessary at high line voltages. The integrated 4A MOSFET provides adequate margin for 30W operation with proper heat sinking.
faqs.decisionGuide: For 30W applications, ensure proper thermal management. Contact us for thermal design guidelines.

faqs.question: How does the quasi-resonant operation work?
faqs.answer: Quasi-resonant (QR) operation switches the MOSFET at the valley of the drain voltage ringing after the transformer demagnetization. This reduces switching losses and EMI by switching at minimum voltage. The STR-A6069H automatically detects the voltage valley and triggers the next switching cycle, optimizing efficiency across load variations. QR operation is particularly beneficial at high line voltages and provides better efficiency than fixed-frequency PWM.
faqs.decisionGuide: QR operation improves efficiency automatically. No external configuration needed for valley detection.

faqs.question: What is the typical application circuit for STR-A6069H?
faqs.answer: The typical application requires the STR-A6069H connected to the primary side of a flyback transformer. The drain pin connects to the transformer primary, source pin to current sense resistor. The feedback pin receives control signal from secondary side through optocoupler or auxiliary winding. External components include startup resistors, snubber circuit, and VCC capacitor. Reference schematics are available showing complete power supply designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request reference designs from our team for your specific application. We provide complete schematics and BOMs.

faqs.question: How do I set the output voltage?
faqs.answer: Output voltage is regulated through the feedback network on the secondary side. Use a TL431 shunt regulator or similar with resistor divider to set the desired output voltage. The feedback signal is coupled to the primary side through an optocoupler connected to the FB pin of STR-A6069H. The controller adjusts the switching to maintain the regulated output voltage. For multiple outputs, cross-regulation techniques may be needed.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard TL431 feedback circuit. Contact us for feedback loop design guidance.

faqs.question: What transformer parameters are recommended?
faqs.answer: Transformer design depends on output power, input voltage range, and switching frequency. For 30W applications, EE16 or EE19 cores are typically used. Primary inductance is calculated based on maximum power and minimum frequency. Turns ratio is determined by output voltage and diode drop. The transformer should be designed with appropriate core cross-section to avoid saturation. Starrystone Tech provides transformer design guidelines and can recommend qualified transformer suppliers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow transformer design guidelines in the datasheet. Contact us for transformer supplier recommendations.

faqs.question: Does STR-A6069H meet energy efficiency standards?
faqs.answer: Yes, STR-A6069H is designed to meet major energy efficiency standards including DoE Level VI (USA), CoC Tier 2 (Europe), and similar regulations. The low standby power (<75mW) and high active mode efficiency help achieve compliance. Actual efficiency depends on the complete power supply design including transformer, output rectifier, and filter components. Reference designs demonstrate compliance with these standards.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow reference designs for standard-compliant implementations. We can provide efficiency test data.

products.name: Synchronous Rectifier Controller
products.shortDescription: High-performance synchronous rectifier controller for flyback converters, replacing diode rectifiers to
descriptionParagraphs: The STR-SR3010 is a synchronous rectifier controller designed for flyback converter secondary sides.
It drives external MOSFETs to replace diode rectifiers, significantly reducing conduction losses.
The controller features intelligent control algorithms for reliable operation across all load conditions.
features: High-current gate drive for fast switching
Light-load mode for high efficiency at low loads
Fast turn-off to prevent reverse current

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: FAE Manager - Power Applications
faeReview.content: The STR-SR3010 delivers excellent performance in synchronous rectification applications. The efficiency improvement of 3-5% over Schottky diodes is significant, especially in thermal-constrained designs. I particularly like the intelligent control algorithm that adapts to different operating modes. The fast turn-off prevents reverse current issues that can plague synchronous rectifier designs. For high-efficiency adapter designs, this controller is a cost-effective solution that pays for itself through reduced thermal management requirements.

alternativeParts.comparison: STR-SR3010=><MP6901: Frequency 150kHz < 300kHz, suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: STR-SR3010 offers similar performance at competitive cost
alternativeParts.useCase: STR-SR3010 for cost-sensitive designs; MP6901 for higher frequency applications

alternativeParts.comparison: STR-SR3010=><NCP4303: Frequency 150kHz < 250kHz, suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: STR-SR3010 provides cost-effective solution with local support
alternativeParts.useCase: STR-SR3010 for standard applications; NCP4303 for high-frequency designs

companionParts.description: Primary side PWM controller for complete power supply
Gate drive resistor for optimal switching

faqs.question: How much efficiency improvement can STR-SR3010 provide?
faqs.answer: STR-SR3010 typically provides 3-5% efficiency improvement over Schottky diode rectifiers, depending on output current and voltage. At higher currents (3A+), the improvement is more significant due to the lower conduction losses of MOSFETs compared to diodes. The efficiency gain translates to reduced heat generation, allowing for smaller heat sinks or higher power density designs. Actual improvement depends on the specific operating conditions and MOSFET selection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Expect 3-5% efficiency improvement. Higher gains at higher output currents.

faqs.question: What MOSFET should I use with STR-SR3010?
faqs.answer: Select MOSFETs with low RDS(on) and appropriate voltage rating. For typical 5V-20V outputs, use 40V-60V MOSFETs with RDS(on) below 5mΩ for best efficiency. Gate charge should be moderate to allow fast switching without excessive drive losses. Popular choices include SOP-8 or DFN packages with dual MOSFETs for compact designs. Consider thermal characteristics as the MOSFET will dissipate power during conduction.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use low-RDS(on) MOSFETs (40V-100V rating). Contact us for specific part recommendations.

faqs.question: How does STR-SR3010 prevent reverse current?
faqs.answer: STR-SR3010 includes fast turn-off detection that monitors the MOSFET drain voltage. When the current attempts to reverse (indicated by drain voltage rising), the controller quickly turns off the MOSFET to block reverse current flow. This prevents energy flow back to the transformer and maintains proper converter operation. The fast response time ensures reliable operation even during transient conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Reverse current protection is automatic. No external components needed.

faqs.question: Can STR-SR3010 work in both CCM and DCM modes?
faqs.answer: Yes, STR-SR3010 supports both Continuous Conduction Mode (CCM) and Discontinuous Conduction Mode (DCM) operation. The controller automatically detects the operating mode and adjusts the control timing accordingly. This makes it suitable for a wide range of flyback designs regardless of the conduction mode. The adaptive control ensures optimal efficiency across different load conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Works in both CCM and DCM automatically. No mode selection needed.

faqs.question: What is the recommended PCB layout for STR-SR3010?
faqs.answer: Proper PCB layout is important for reliable operation: Place the controller close to the synchronous MOSFET to minimize gate drive loop inductance. Keep the current sense connections short and away from switching nodes. Provide adequate copper area for the MOSFET drain connection to handle current and dissipate heat. Connect the VCC decoupling capacitor close to the IC. Follow the layout guidelines in the datasheet for optimal performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow layout guidelines in datasheet. Minimize gate drive loop area.

faqs.question: Does STR-SR3010 require a specific primary side controller?
faqs.answer: STR-SR3010 works with any flyback PWM controller - it operates independently on the secondary side. It can be paired with Starrystone Tech STR-A series controllers or controllers from other manufacturers. The synchronous rectifier controller monitors transformer demagnetization through the MOSFET drain voltage and does not require communication with the primary side controller. This makes it easy to add synchronous rectification to existing designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Works with any flyback controller. Can retrofit existing designs.

categories.name: DC-DC Converters
categories.description: High-efficiency DC-DC converters including buck, boost, and buck-boost topologies for various voltage conversion applications.
categories.longDescription: Starrystone Tech's DC-DC converter portfolio provides efficient power conversion solutions for applications requiring voltage step-down, step-up, or both. The product range includes synchronous buck converters for high-efficiency step-down applications, boost converters for step-up requirements, and buck-boost converters for applications where input voltage may be above or below the output. These converters feature high switching frequencies, allowing compact designs with small external components. With integrated power switches and comprehensive protection features, Starrystone Tech DC-DC converters simplify design while providing excellent performance. LiTong is your authorized distributor providing selection guidance and technical support for Starrystone Tech products.

series.name: STR-B Series
series.description: Synchronous buck converters for step-down applications

series.name: STR-C Series
series.description: Buck-boost converters for wide input range applications

selectionGuide.title: How to Select DC-DC Converters
selectionGuide.description: Consider input/output voltage, current requirements, topology, and efficiency needs

faqs.question: What is the difference between buck, boost, and buck-boost converters?
faqs.answer: Buck converters step down voltage (output < input) with high efficiency. Boost converters step up voltage (output > input). Buck-boost converters can both step up and step down, making them suitable for applications where input voltage varies above and below the output (like battery-powered systems). Starrystone Tech offers all three types: STR-B series for buck, STR-U series for boost, and STR-C series for buck-boost applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select topology based on input/output voltage relationship. Contact us for topology selection guidance.

faqs.question: What is the efficiency of Starrystone Tech DC-DC converters?
faqs.answer: Starrystone Tech DC-DC converters achieve high efficiency, typically 90-95% at moderate to heavy loads. Synchronous rectification eliminates diode losses, improving efficiency especially at low output voltages. Efficiency varies with input voltage, output voltage, and load current. Light-load efficiency is optimized through features like automatic PFM mode switching. Refer to efficiency curves in datasheets for specific operating conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: For battery-powered applications, efficiency directly impacts runtime. Review efficiency curves for your operating conditions.

faqs.question: What protection features are included in Starrystone Tech DC-DC converters?
faqs.answer: Starrystone Tech DC-DC converters include comprehensive protection features: over-current protection (OCP) prevents damage from excessive load current; over-voltage protection (OVP) safeguards against output voltage faults; under-voltage lockout (UVLO) ensures proper startup; over-temperature protection (OTP) prevents thermal damage; and short-circuit protection (SCP) handles output faults. These protections ensure reliable operation and protect both the IC and connected circuits.
faqs.decisionGuide: The integrated protections simplify design and improve reliability. Review datasheet for specific protection thresholds.

faqs.question: How do I select the right inductor for DC-DC converters?
faqs.answer: Inductor selection affects efficiency, ripple current, and transient response. Choose inductance value based on switching frequency and desired ripple current (typically 20-40% of output current). Higher inductance reduces ripple but may slow transient response. Consider saturation current rating (should exceed maximum load plus ripple) and DCR (affects efficiency). Shielded inductors reduce EMI. Starrystone Tech datasheets provide recommended inductor values and part numbers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow datasheet recommendations for inductor selection. Contact our FAE team for optimization guidance.

faqs.question: What package options are available for Starrystone Tech DC-DC converters?
faqs.answer: Starrystone Tech DC-DC converters are available in compact packages suitable for space-constrained applications. Common packages include SOT23-5/6, SOP-8, ESOP-8, and QFN (2x2mm to 4x4mm). The small packages enable high power density designs for portable electronics and compact modules. Thermal performance varies by package, with larger packages offering better heat dissipation. Select package based on space constraints, thermal requirements, and manufacturing considerations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose package based on space and thermal requirements. Review PCB layout guidelines in datasheets.

products.name: 3A Synchronous Buck Converter
products.shortDescription: High-efficiency synchronous buck converter with 3A output current, wide input range, and compact SOT23-5 package
descriptionParagraphs: The STR-B3401 is a high-efficiency synchronous buck converter capable of delivering up to 3A output current.
It features a wide input voltage range and integrated high and low-side MOSFETs for high efficiency.
The compact SOT23-5 package makes it ideal for space-constrained applications.

faeReview.author: Lisa Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Management
faeReview.content: The STR-B3401 is a versatile buck converter that delivers excellent performance in a tiny package. The 3A capability in SOT23-5 is impressive and makes it perfect for compact designs. The efficiency is consistently high across the load range, and the internal compensation really simplifies the design process - no need to calculate external compensation components. I've used this in numerous designs from set-top boxes to industrial modules, and it performs reliably every time. For designers needing high current in limited space, this is an excellent choice.
faeReview.highlight: 3A capability in compact SOT23-5 package with 95% efficiency

alternativeParts.reason: STR-B3401 offers competitive performance at lower cost
alternativeParts.useCase: STR-B3401 for cost-sensitive designs; MP1584 for higher input voltage

alternativeParts.comparison: STR-B3401=><TPS54331: Package SOT23-5 < SOIC-8 (smaller), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: STR-B3401 offers smaller package at competitive cost
alternativeParts.useCase: STR-B3401 for compact designs; TPS54331 for wider input range

companionParts.description: 2A buck converter for lower current rails
Low ESR capacitors for output filtering

faqs.question: What is the maximum output current of STR-B3401?
faqs.answer: The STR-B3401 is rated for 3A maximum output current. The actual continuous current capability depends on input voltage, output voltage, ambient temperature, and thermal design. At higher input-output voltage differentials, thermal limitations may reduce maximum current. The integrated MOSFETs have low RDS(on) to minimize conduction losses. For reliable operation, ensure adequate copper area for heat dissipation and consider derating at high ambient temperatures.
faqs.decisionGuide: 3A rated current. Ensure proper thermal design for continuous operation at maximum current.

faqs.question: How do I set the output voltage?
faqs.answer: Output voltage is set using external resistor dividers connected to the FB pin. The feedback reference voltage is 0.8V. Calculate resistor values using: Vout = 0.8V × (1 + R1/R2). For example, for 3.3V output: R1/R2 = (3.3/0.8) - 1 = 3.125. Use standard values like R1=22kΩ, R2=6.8kΩ. Place feedback resistors close to the IC and keep FB trace short. The datasheet provides recommended values for common output voltages.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use resistor dividers with 0.8V reference. Place close to IC for noise immunity.

faqs.question: What input capacitors are recommended?
faqs.answer: Use ceramic capacitors with X5R or X7R dielectric for input filtering. Recommended value is 10μF or larger, rated for the maximum input voltage with margin. Place the input capacitor as close as possible to the VIN and GND pins to minimize switching loop area. For applications with long input leads or significant input inductance, add additional bulk capacitance (47-100μF electrolytic) to prevent input voltage ringing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 10μF+ ceramic capacitors close to IC. Add bulk capacitance if needed for long leads.

faqs.question: Can STR-B3401 operate at 100% duty cycle?
faqs.answer: The STR-B3401 has a maximum duty cycle limit (typically 90-95%) to ensure proper bootstrap capacitor charging for the high-side MOSFET driver. This means the output voltage cannot be equal to the input voltage - there will always be some dropout voltage. For applications requiring dropout operation (Vout ≈ Vin), consider using a buck converter with 100% duty cycle capability or an LDO for the dropout region.
faqs.decisionGuide: Maximum 90-95% duty cycle. Consider dropout requirements in design.

faqs.question: What is the switching frequency of STR-B3401?
faqs.answer: The STR-B3401 operates at a fixed 500kHz switching frequency. This frequency provides a good balance between efficiency, component size, and EMI. The 500kHz allows reasonably small inductors while maintaining good efficiency. The fixed frequency simplifies EMI filter design compared to variable frequency converters. For applications requiring different frequencies, consider other models in the Starrystone Tech portfolio.
faqs.decisionGuide: Fixed 500kHz operation. Suitable for most applications. Consider EMI in PCB layout.

faqs.question: How do I calculate power dissipation and thermal design?
faqs.answer: Power dissipation can be estimated using: Pd = Iout × (1 - Efficiency) × Vout/Vin, or more accurately by calculating conduction and switching losses. Conduction loss = Iout² × RDS(on) × duty cycle. Switching loss depends on switching frequency and transition times. For thermal design, ensure junction temperature stays below 125°C. Calculate temperature rise: ΔT = Pd × θja, where θja is thermal resistance. Provide adequate copper area (typically 20-50mm² for 1-2W dissipation).
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate losses and ensure Tj < 125°C. Provide adequate copper area for heat dissipation.

products.name: Buck-Boost Converter
products.shortDescription: High-efficiency buck-boost converter with seamless mode transition, ideal for battery-powered applications with
descriptionParagraphs: The STR-C3501 is a synchronous buck-boost converter designed for applications with variable input voltage.
It seamlessly transitions between buck and boost modes as input voltage varies above and below the output.
The IC is ideal for battery-powered applications where input voltage changes during discharge.

faeReview.author: Robert Zhang
faeReview.title: FAE - Portable Power Solutions
faeReview.content: The STR-C3501 is an excellent choice for battery-powered designs requiring stable output regardless of battery voltage. The seamless mode transition is truly seamless - there's no glitch or instability when crossing the buck-boost boundary. I've used this in several single-cell lithium battery designs where the output needs to be regulated at 3.3V or 5V as the battery discharges from 4.2V to 3.0V. The efficiency remains high across the entire battery voltage range. The 1MHz switching allows for compact inductor size. For battery-powered products, this converter provides reliable, efficient performance.
faeReview.highlight: Seamless buck-boost with wide input range for battery applications

alternativeParts.reason: STR-C3501 supports wider voltage range and higher output voltage
alternativeParts.useCase: STR-C3501 for wide-range applications; TPS63020 for low-voltage designs

alternativeParts.comparison: STR-C3501=><LTC3780: Package QFN-16 < TSSOP-24 (smaller), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: STR-C3501 offers compact solution for lower power applications
alternativeParts.useCase: STR-C3501 for compact sub-15W designs; LTC3780 for high-power applications

companionParts.description: Buck converter for additional voltage rails
Low ESR capacitors for output filtering

faqs.question: How does the seamless buck-boost transition work?
faqs.answer: The STR-C3501 uses a 4-switch H-bridge topology that automatically reconfigures based on the input-output voltage relationship. When Vin > Vout, it operates in buck mode with the buck switches active. When Vin < Vout, it operates in boost mode with the boost switches active. The transition between modes happens automatically based on voltage comparison, with no output disturbance. The control loop maintains stable output regulation throughout the entire input voltage range.
faqs.decisionGuide: Seamless transition is automatic. No external control needed. Ideal for battery applications.

faqs.question: What battery configurations work with STR-C3501?
faqs.answer: STR-C3501 works with various battery configurations due to its wide 2.5V-14V input range. For single-cell lithium (3.0V-4.2V), it can produce regulated 3.3V or 5V output. For 2S lithium (6V-8.4V), it can produce 5V or 12V output. For 3S lithium (9V-12.6V), it can produce 12V output. The converter maintains regulation throughout the battery discharge curve, maximizing usable battery capacity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Works with 1S to 3S lithium batteries. Select output voltage based on application needs.

faqs.question: What is the efficiency in buck vs boost mode?
faqs.answer: Efficiency varies between buck and boost modes due to different current paths and switch configurations. Buck mode typically achieves slightly higher efficiency (93-95%) because current flows through fewer switches. Boost mode efficiency is typically 90-93% due to higher switch currents. The efficiency is optimized for each mode through the control algorithm. Overall efficiency across the input voltage range remains high, making it suitable for battery applications where input voltage varies.
faqs.decisionGuide: Efficiency is high in both modes. Expect 90-95% depending on operating point.

faqs.question: How do I set the output voltage on STR-C3501?
faqs.answer: Output voltage is set using external resistor dividers connected to the FB pin. The feedback reference voltage is typically 1.0V or 0.8V depending on the specific variant. Calculate resistor values using the standard voltage divider formula. Place feedback resistors close to the IC and keep the FB trace short. The datasheet provides recommended resistor values for common output voltages. Some variants may support I2C or other digital interfaces for voltage adjustment.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use resistor dividers for fixed outputs. Check datasheet for reference voltage.

faqs.question: What inductor value should I use?
faqs.answer: The recommended inductor value depends on the switching frequency and desired ripple current. For the STR-C3501's 1MHz switching frequency, a 2.2μH inductor is typically recommended for 2A applications. The inductor should have saturation current rating above the maximum peak current (typically 1.2-1.5x output current). Low DCR is important for efficiency. Shielded inductors are recommended to minimize EMI. The datasheet provides detailed inductor selection guidelines.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 2.2μH for typical applications. Ensure adequate saturation current rating.

faqs.question: Can STR-C3501 handle load transients well?
faqs.answer: Yes, STR-C3501 includes internal compensation optimized for good transient response. The 1MHz switching frequency allows fast response to load changes. For applications with large load transients, additional output capacitance may be needed to limit voltage droop or overshoot. The power good (PG) output can be used to signal when the output is in regulation after a transient event. For critical applications, test transient response under actual operating conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Good transient response with internal compensation. Add output capacitance for large load steps.

categories.name: LED Drivers
categories.description: Comprehensive LED driver solutions for general lighting, commercial lighting, and display backlighting applications.
categories.longDescription: Starrystone Tech's LED driver portfolio provides complete solutions for LED lighting applications. The product range includes buck LED drivers for step-down configurations, boost LED drivers for step-up requirements, and buck-boost drivers for wide input range applications. These drivers feature constant current regulation for consistent LED brightness, dimming control for brightness adjustment, and comprehensive protection features for reliable operation. With support for various LED configurations and power levels, Starrystone Tech LED drivers suit applications ranging from small indicator LEDs to high-power lighting systems. LiTong is your authorized distributor providing selection guidance and technical support for Starrystone Tech products.

series.name: STR-L Series
series.description: LED drivers for general lighting applications

series.name: STR-H Series
series.description: High-power LED drivers for commercial lighting

selectionGuide.title: How to Select LED Drivers
selectionGuide.description: Consider LED configuration, current requirements, dimming needs, and topology

faqs.question: What LED driver topologies does Starrystone Tech offer?
faqs.answer: Starrystone Tech offers LED drivers in multiple topologies to suit different LED configurations. Buck (step-down) drivers are used when input voltage is higher than LED string voltage. Boost (step-up) drivers are used when LED string voltage exceeds input voltage. Buck-boost drivers handle both situations and are ideal for battery-powered applications. The STR-L series covers low to medium power, while STR-H series handles high-power applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select topology based on input voltage and LED string voltage relationship.

faqs.question: What dimming methods are supported?
faqs.answer: Starrystone Tech LED drivers support multiple dimming methods. Analog dimming adjusts LED current proportionally to a control voltage (typically 0-10V or 0-1V). PWM dimming switches the LED current on/off at high frequency with variable duty cycle for brightness control. Some drivers support digital interfaces like I2C for precise brightness control. The choice depends on application requirements - analog is simpler, PWM maintains color temperature, digital offers most flexibility.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose dimming method based on control system. PWM recommended for maintaining color temperature.

faqs.question: How do I calculate the LED current setting?
faqs.answer: LED current is typically set using a current sense resistor. The driver regulates the voltage across this resistor to maintain constant current. Calculate resistor value using: Rsense = Vref / Iled, where Vref is the driver's reference voltage (typically 0.2V-0.25V) and Iled is desired LED current. For example, for 350mA LED current with 0.25V reference: Rsense = 0.25V / 0.35A = 0.714Ω. Use standard resistor values and consider power rating (P = I² × R).
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate based on reference voltage and desired current. Check datasheet for specific reference voltage.

faqs.question: What protection features do Starrystone Tech LED drivers include?
faqs.answer: Starrystone Tech LED drivers include comprehensive protection features: LED open protection prevents over-voltage if LED string opens; LED short protection limits current if LEDs short; over-current protection prevents excessive driver current; over-temperature protection reduces current or shuts down if overheating; and under-voltage lockout ensures proper startup. These protections ensure reliable operation and long LED lifetime.
faqs.decisionGuide: Integrated protections ensure reliability. Review datasheet for specific protection thresholds.

faqs.question: Can Starrystone Tech LED drivers support multiple LED strings?
faqs.answer: Some Starrystone Tech LED driver models support multiple LED strings with current balancing. This is useful for applications requiring multiple LED channels or redundancy. Current balancing ensures equal current in each string despite variations in LED forward voltage. Check specific product datasheets for multi-string capability. For applications requiring independent control of multiple strings, multiple driver ICs may be needed.
faqs.decisionGuide: Check datasheet for multi-string support. Use multiple drivers for independent string control.

products.name: Buck LED Driver
products.shortDescription: High-efficiency buck LED driver with wide input range, analog and PWM dimming support for general lighting
descriptionParagraphs: The STR-L3201 is a high-efficiency buck LED driver designed for general lighting applications.
It provides constant current regulation with analog and PWM dimming control.
The wide input voltage range makes it suitable for various input sources including AC-DC adapters and DC supplies.

faeReview.author: Kevin Liu
faeReview.title: FAE - Lighting Solutions
faeReview.content: The STR-L3201 is a versatile LED driver that works well in a wide range of lighting applications. The dual dimming options (analog and PWM) provide flexibility for different control systems. The 96% efficiency helps minimize heat generation in the driver, which is important for thermally constrained lighting fixtures. I've used this driver in numerous downlight and bulb designs with excellent results. The protection features are reliable - I've seen it properly handle LED open and short conditions without damage. For general lighting up to 1A, this is a solid, cost-effective choice.
faeReview.highlight: Dual dimming with 96% efficiency for versatile lighting applications

alternativeParts.reason: STR-L3201 offers analog dimming and wider voltage range at competitive cost
alternativeParts.useCase: STR-L3201 for analog dimming needs; MP24833 for higher current

alternativeParts.reason: STR-L3201 provides cost-effective solution for standard applications
alternativeParts.useCase: STR-L3201 for cost-sensitive designs; TPS92512 for advanced features

faqs.question: How many LEDs can STR-L3201 drive?
faqs.answer: The number of LEDs depends on the LED forward voltage and input voltage. STR-L3201 supports LED string voltage up to 36V. For typical white LEDs with 3V forward voltage, this allows up to 10-12 LEDs in series. For higher voltage LEDs or longer strings, ensure total LED voltage drop is below the maximum. The driver maintains constant current through the LED string regardless of the number of LEDs (within voltage limits).
faqs.decisionGuide: Maximum 36V LED string voltage. Calculate based on LED forward voltage.

faqs.question: How do I set the LED current?
faqs.answer: LED current is set using a current sense resistor between the CS pin and ground. The formula is: Rsense = Vcs / Iled, where Vcs is the current sense threshold voltage (typically 0.25V) and Iled is the desired LED current. For example, for 350mA LED current: Rsense = 0.25V / 0.35A = 0.714Ω. Use a standard value like 0.68Ω or 0.75Ω. The resistor power rating should be at least 2x the calculated power dissipation (P = I² × R).
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate using sense threshold voltage. Check datasheet for exact Vcs value.

faqs.question: What is the difference between analog and PWM dimming?
faqs.answer: Analog dimming varies the LED current proportionally to a control voltage (0-2.5V for STR-L3201). Lower current reduces brightness but may shift LED color temperature slightly. PWM dimming switches the LED on/off rapidly (typically 200Hz-1kHz) with variable duty cycle. PWM maintains consistent LED color temperature across brightness levels since LEDs operate at full current when on. Analog is simpler to implement; PWM is better for color-critical applications and wider dimming range.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use PWM for color-critical applications. Use analog for simpler implementations.

faqs.question: How do I implement PWM dimming?
faqs.answer: For PWM dimming, apply a PWM signal (200Hz-1kHz recommended) to the DIM pin of STR-L3201. The duty cycle controls brightness: 100% duty = full brightness, 0% duty = off, 50% duty = half brightness. The PWM frequency should be high enough to avoid visible flicker (>200Hz) but not so high that switching losses become significant. The DIM pin typically accepts 3.3V or 5V logic levels. Connect the PWM source directly to the DIM pin.
faqs.decisionGuide: Apply 200Hz-1kHz PWM to DIM pin. Use 3.3V or 5V logic levels.

faqs.question: What happens if the LED string opens?
faqs.answer: STR-L3201 includes LED open protection. If the LED string opens (disconnected or failed), the driver detects the over-voltage condition and enters protection mode to prevent damage. The driver will typically shut down or enter a hiccup mode, retrying periodically. This protection prevents the output capacitor from charging to excessive voltage when no LED load is present. Once the LED fault is cleared, normal operation resumes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Open protection is automatic. No external components needed.

faqs.question: Can I parallel multiple STR-L3201 drivers for higher current?
faqs.answer: While it's possible to parallel LED drivers, it's generally not recommended due to current sharing issues. Each driver will regulate its own current, but slight variations in reference voltage can cause uneven current distribution. For higher current applications, it's better to use a single higher-current driver (like STR-L3202 for 2A) or split the LED string into multiple independent strings, each with its own driver. This ensures proper current regulation in each string.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use higher current driver or multiple independent strings instead of paralleling.

products.name: High-Power LED Driver
products.shortDescription: High-power boost LED driver supporting up to 60V output for long LED strings in commercial and industrial lighting
descriptionParagraphs: The STR-H5601 is a high-power boost LED driver designed for commercial and industrial lighting applications.
It supports long LED strings with output voltage up to 60V and output current up to 2A.
The driver features comprehensive protection and multiple dimming options for professional lighting systems.

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Lighting
faeReview.content: The STR-H5601 is designed for demanding commercial and industrial lighting applications. The 60V output capability allows driving long LED strings common in street lights and high-bay fixtures. The 100W power handling is impressive for the compact package. I particularly like the comprehensive protection suite - it's essential for outdoor and industrial applications where reliability is critical. The multiple dimming options provide flexibility for smart lighting systems. For professional lighting applications, this driver delivers the performance and reliability needed.
faeReview.highlight: 100W capability with 60V output for commercial lighting

alternativeParts.reason: STR-H5601 provides cost-effective solution for standard high-power applications
alternativeParts.useCase: STR-H5601 for cost-sensitive designs; TPS92692 for advanced features

alternativeParts.comparison: STR-H5601=><AL8843: Package ESOP-10 vs SO-8, suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: STR-H5601 supports higher output voltage for longer LED strings
alternativeParts.useCase: STR-H5601 for high-voltage applications; AL8843 for lower voltage, higher current

companionParts.description: Lower power driver for auxiliary lighting

faqs.question: How many LEDs can STR-H5601 drive in series?
faqs.answer: STR-H5601 supports LED string voltage up to 60V. For typical white LEDs with 3V forward voltage, this allows up to 18-20 LEDs in series. For higher forward voltage LEDs (some high-power LEDs have 6-12V Vf), fewer LEDs can be driven. The high output voltage capability makes this driver ideal for applications requiring long LED strings such as street lights and linear high-bay fixtures. Calculate based on your specific LED forward voltage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Maximum 60V LED string. Calculate LED count based on forward voltage.

faqs.question: What input voltage is required for boost operation?
faqs.answer: For boost operation, input voltage must be lower than the LED string voltage. The STR-H5601 input range is 9V-36V. For example, to drive a 48V LED string, input voltage must be below 48V (typically 24V or 36V). The boost ratio (Vout/Vin) should typically not exceed 3-4 for stable operation and good efficiency. For very high boost ratios, consider alternative topologies or input voltage adjustments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Input must be lower than output. Limit boost ratio to 3-4 for best performance.

faqs.question: How do I manage thermal design for high-power operation?
faqs.answer: Thermal design is critical for high-power LED drivers. Calculate power dissipation: Pd = Pin - Pout = (Vout × Iout / Efficiency) - (Vout × Iout). For 100W output at 95% efficiency, dissipation is about 5W. Provide adequate copper area on PCB (typically 50-100mm² for 5W), use thermal vias to inner layers, and consider external heat sinking if needed. Monitor case temperature during testing and ensure it stays within ratings. The ESOP-10 package has an exposed pad for improved heat dissipation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate dissipation and provide adequate copper area. Use exposed pad for heat sinking.

faqs.question: What is the difference between boost and buck-boost LED drivers?
faqs.answer: Boost drivers (like STR-H5601) can only step up voltage - output must be higher than input. They're used when LED string voltage exceeds the supply voltage. Buck-boost drivers can both step up and step down, making them suitable when input voltage may be above or below LED voltage (like battery-powered applications). Boost drivers are generally more efficient for pure step-up applications and can achieve higher output voltages. Choose based on your input/output voltage relationship.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use boost for step-up only. Use buck-boost if input can be above or below LED voltage.

faqs.question: Can STR-H5601 be used for multi-string LED applications?
faqs.answer: STR-H5601 is designed for single LED string operation. For multi-string applications requiring independent current control for each string, multiple driver ICs are recommended. This ensures proper current regulation in each string despite LED forward voltage variations. If the strings have identical LED configurations and can share the same current, a single driver with appropriate current rating can be used, but current balancing between strings is not guaranteed.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use multiple drivers for independent string control. Single driver only if strings can share current.

faqs.question: What safety certifications are needed for LED drivers?
faqs.answer: LED drivers typically require safety certifications including UL8750 (USA) for LED equipment, CE marking (Europe), and CCC (China). Additional certifications may be required depending on application - for example, street lighting may need specific outdoor ratings. The driver design should meet isolation requirements, creepage/clearance distances, and temperature limits. Starrystone Tech designs incorporate safety considerations, but final product certification is the manufacturer's responsibility.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan for UL8750 and CE certifications. Engage with testing labs early in design.

categories.name: Gate Drivers
categories.description: High and low-side gate drivers for power MOSFET and IGBT switches in motor drives, power supplies, and inverters.
categories.longDescription: Starrystone Tech's gate driver portfolio provides efficient control of power MOSFETs and IGBTs in switching applications. The product range includes low-side drivers for ground-referenced switches, high-side drivers for floating switches, and half-bridge drivers for synchronous buck/boost and motor drive applications. These drivers feature fast switching speeds, high peak drive current, and robust protection features. With wide operating voltage ranges and excellent noise immunity, Starrystone Tech gate drivers suit applications from small DC-DC converters to high-power motor drives and inverters. LiTong is your authorized distributor providing selection guidance and technical support for Starrystone Tech products.

series.name: STR-G Series
series.description: Low-side gate drivers for ground-referenced switches

series.name: STR-HB Series
series.description: Half-bridge drivers for synchronous converters and motor drives

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Gate Drivers
selectionGuide.description: Consider switch type, voltage rating, drive current, and topology requirements

faqs.question: What is the difference between low-side and high-side gate drivers?
faqs.answer: Low-side gate drivers control switches with source connected to ground. They're simpler and can use ground-referenced power supplies. High-side drivers control switches with source floating at the switching node (above ground). They require bootstrap or isolated power supplies and level-shifting circuits. Half-bridge drivers combine both - one low-side and one high-side driver for controlling two switches in a half-bridge configuration. Choose based on your circuit topology.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use low-side for ground-referenced switches. Use half-bridge for synchronous converters and motor drives.

faqs.question: How do I calculate the required gate drive current?
faqs.answer: Gate drive current determines switching speed. Calculate using: Igate = Qg / t, where Qg is total gate charge of the MOSFET/IGBT and t is desired switching time. For example, if Qg = 50nC and you want 50ns switching: Igate = 50nC / 50ns = 1A. The driver must provide this peak current. Faster switching reduces switching losses but increases EMI and voltage ringing. Select a driver with adequate current capability for your switching speed requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate based on gate charge and desired switching time. Select driver with adequate peak current.

faqs.question: What is bootstrap power supply in half-bridge drivers?
faqs.answer: Bootstrap power supply generates the high-side gate drive voltage using a diode and capacitor. When the low-side switch is on, the bootstrap capacitor charges through the diode from the low-side supply. When the low-side switch turns off and high-side turns on, the charged capacitor provides floating power for the high-side driver. This eliminates the need for isolated power supplies. The bootstrap capacitor must be sized appropriately and refreshed regularly (minimum duty cycle or periodic low-side on-time required).
faqs.decisionGuide: Bootstrap is standard for half-bridge drivers. Size capacitor appropriately for switching frequency.

faqs.question: What protection features do gate drivers include?
faqs.answer: Starrystone Tech gate drivers include protection features such as under-voltage lockout (UVLO) to prevent operation with insufficient gate voltage, shoot-through prevention logic in half-bridge drivers to avoid simultaneous conduction, over-temperature protection, and fault reporting outputs. Some drivers include desaturation detection for IGBT protection. These features ensure reliable operation and protect the power switches from damage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Integrated protections improve reliability. Review datasheet for specific protection features.

faqs.question: How do I minimize switching noise and EMI?
faqs.answer: Minimize switching noise through proper PCB layout: Keep gate drive loops small and tight; Use Kelvin connections for gate drive; Place decoupling capacitors close to driver IC; Minimize parasitic inductance in high di/dt paths; Use appropriate gate resistors to control switching speed; Implement snubbers if needed for voltage ringing. Balance switching speed (efficiency) against EMI - slower switching reduces EMI but increases switching losses.
faqs.decisionGuide: Optimize PCB layout and use gate resistors to control switching speed. Trade-off between efficiency and EMI.

products.name: Dual Low-Side Gate Driver
products.shortDescription: High-speed dual low-side gate driver with 4A peak output current, suitable for driving power MOSFETs and IGBTs in
descriptionParagraphs: The STR-G4401 is a high-speed dual low-side gate driver designed for driving power switches.
It provides 4A peak drive current for fast switching of MOSFETs and IGBTs.
The dual channels can be used independently or together for driving two switches.

faeReview.author: Alex Wang
faeReview.title: FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The STR-G4401 is a reliable dual gate driver that delivers consistent performance. The 4A drive current is sufficient for most MOSFETs up to medium power levels. I appreciate the fast switching times and good delay matching between channels - important for synchronous rectifier applications. The wide voltage range allows flexibility in drive voltage selection. I've used this driver in numerous DC-DC converter and motor drive designs. For cost-sensitive applications requiring dual channels, this is a solid choice.
faeReview.highlight: Dual 4A channels with fast switching and good matching

alternativeParts.reason: STR-G4401 offers similar performance at competitive cost

alternativeParts.reason: STR-G4401 provides higher drive current and more features
alternativeParts.useCase: STR-G4401 for higher performance; IRS4427 for basic applications

companionParts.description: Fast recovery diode for bootstrap circuit

faqs.question: What gate resistor value should I use?
faqs.answer: Gate resistor selection balances switching speed against EMI and ringing. Typical values range from 1Ω to 47Ω. Lower values (1-10Ω) provide fast switching for high-efficiency applications but may cause more ringing. Higher values (22-47Ω) slow switching for reduced EMI but increase switching losses. Start with 10Ω and adjust based on waveform measurements. Some designs use different values for turn-on and turn-off (asymmetric drive) to optimize both characteristics.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with 10Ω and optimize based on switching waveforms. Consider asymmetric drive for optimization.

faqs.question: Can I parallel the two channels for higher drive current?
faqs.answer: Yes, the two channels of STR-G4401 can be paralleled for 8A peak drive capability. Connect the inputs together and the outputs together. The delay matching specification (5ns) ensures both channels switch simultaneously for effective current sharing. This is useful for driving large MOSFETs or IGBTs with high gate charge. Ensure proper PCB layout for current sharing - keep traces symmetric and minimize inductance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Can parallel for 8A total. Ensure symmetric layout for current sharing.

faqs.question: What is the difference between source and sink current?
faqs.answer: Source current (also called pull-up or turn-on) flows from the driver to the gate to charge the gate capacitance and turn on the MOSFET. Sink current (also called pull-down or turn-off) flows from the gate to the driver to discharge the gate capacitance and turn off the MOSFET. STR-G4401 provides 4A for both source and sink, ensuring fast turn-on and turn-off. Some applications may benefit from different turn-on and turn-off speeds, achieved by using different external gate resistors.
faqs.decisionGuide: 4A source and sink for fast switching. Use different gate resistors for asymmetric switching.

faqs.question: How do I calculate power dissipation in the gate driver?
faqs.answer: Gate driver power dissipation comes from charging/discharging gate capacitance: Pd = Qg × Vdrive × fsw, where Qg is total gate charge, Vdrive is gate drive voltage, and fsw is switching frequency. For example, with Qg=50nC, Vdrive=12V, fsw=100kHz: Pd = 50nC × 12V × 100kHz = 60mW per switch. For dual channels: 120mW total. This is typically low, but consider it for thermal calculations in high-frequency applications or when paralleling channels.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate using gate charge, drive voltage, and frequency. Typically low power dissipation.

faqs.question: What is the UVLO threshold and why is it important?
faqs.answer: UVLO (Under-Voltage Lockout) prevents the driver from operating when supply voltage is too low to properly enhance the MOSFET. STR-G4401 typically has UVLO thresholds around 4.0V (falling) and 4.5V (rising). If supply voltage drops below threshold, the driver outputs are disabled (pulled low). This prevents the MOSFET from operating in linear mode with high resistance, which would cause excessive heating. UVLO ensures reliable operation and protects the power switch.
faqs.decisionGuide: UVLO is automatic. Ensure supply voltage stays above UVLO threshold during operation.

faqs.question: Can STR-G4401 drive IGBTs as well as MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: Yes, STR-G4401 can drive both MOSFETs and IGBTs. The 4A drive current and 18V maximum drive voltage are suitable for most IGBT modules. For IGBTs, ensure the drive voltage is appropriate (typically +15V for turn-on, 0V or -5 to -8V for turn-off). The negative turn-off voltage helps prevent false turn-on due to dV/dt. The driver features are compatible with both device types. Consider the specific IGBT's gate charge and switching requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for both MOSFETs and IGBTs. Adjust drive voltage for IGBT requirements.

The integrated bootstrap supply simplifies the high-side power supply design. I particularly appreciate the adjustable dead time feature - it allows optimization for different MOSFETs and switching frequencies. The shoot-through protection is essential for preventing catastrophic failures. For half-bridge and motor drive applications, this driver provides good performance at a competitive price point.

companionParts.description: Dual low-side driver for additional channels
High and low-side MOSFETs for half-bridge
Fast recovery diode for bootstrap circuit

faqs.question: How does the bootstrap circuit work?
faqs.answer: The bootstrap circuit generates high-side drive voltage using a capacitor and diode. When the low-side switch is on, the bootstrap capacitor charges through the diode from VCC to ground. When the low-side turns off and high-side turns on, the charged capacitor provides floating power referenced to the switching node (VS pin). The capacitor must supply the high-side quiescent current and gate charge without excessive voltage droop. Size the capacitor appropriately (typically 0.1-1μF) based on switching frequency and gate charge.
faqs.decisionGuide: Bootstrap is standard operation. Size capacitor based on frequency and gate charge.

faqs.question: What is dead time and why is it important?
faqs.answer: Dead time is a brief delay between turning off one switch and turning on the other in a half-bridge. It's essential to prevent shoot-through (both switches conducting simultaneously), which would cause a short circuit across the supply. STR-HB5601 provides adjustable dead time (100ns-1μs). The optimal dead time depends on MOSFET switching characteristics - long enough to ensure complete turn-off, short enough to minimize body diode conduction losses. Start with 300-500ns and adjust based on measurements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Set dead time to prevent shoot-through. Start with 300-500ns and optimize.

faqs.question: What is the minimum duty cycle or on-time required?
faqs.answer: The high-side bootstrap capacitor needs periodic refreshing. The low-side switch must be on long enough to recharge the bootstrap capacitor. This sets a minimum duty cycle or minimum low-side on-time requirement. Typically, the low-side must be on for at least a few microseconds every switching cycle or every few cycles. For very low duty cycles or long high-side on-times, consider using an isolated power supply instead of bootstrap, or implement a refresh pulse periodically.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure adequate low-side on-time for bootstrap refresh. Use isolated supply for very low duty cycles.

faqs.question: How do I select the bootstrap capacitor value?
faqs.answer: Calculate bootstrap capacitor using: Cboot = (2 × Qg + Qls + Iq/fsw) / ΔV, where Qg is high-side MOSFET gate charge, Qls is level-shift charge, Iq is high-side quiescent current, fsw is switching frequency, and ΔV is allowed voltage droop (typically 0.5-1V). For most applications, 0.1μF to 1μF is sufficient. Use a low-ESR ceramic capacitor (X5R or X7R) rated for at least 25V. Place the capacitor close to the HB and HS pins.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate based on gate charge and frequency. Use 0.1-1μF ceramic capacitor.

faqs.question: What is shoot-through protection?
faqs.answer: Shoot-through protection prevents both high-side and low-side switches from conducting simultaneously. STR-HB5601 includes logic that ensures one switch is fully off before the other turns on, implementing the dead time. This protection is automatic and prevents catastrophic short-circuit conditions. Even with input signals that would command both switches on, the driver's internal logic prevents simultaneous conduction. This is essential for reliable half-bridge operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Shoot-through protection is automatic. Dead time is adjustable for optimization.

faqs.question: Can STR-HB5601 be used for full-bridge applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, two STR-HB5601 drivers can be used to create a full-bridge (H-bridge) configuration. Each driver controls one half-bridge leg (two switches). The inputs can be driven with complementary PWM signals for full-bridge operation. Ensure proper dead time between diagonal switches to prevent shoot-through. The 600V rating supports high-voltage full-bridge applications like motor drives and inverters. Connect the logic grounds together while keeping power grounds separate as needed.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use two drivers for full-bridge. Ensure proper dead time and grounding.

products.name: Gate Drivers SGD1022
descriptionParagraphs: The SGD1022 is Dual-channel IGBT gate driver. This device features advanced technology for superior performance and reliability.
With IGBT Driver, this device is ideal for demanding applications including industrial systems, consumer electronics, and automotive applications.
The SGD1022 delivers industry-leading performance with excellent thermal characteristics and proven reliability in field applications.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: Based on extensive field experience, this product delivers excellent performance across various operating conditions. The design incorporates proven architecture with robust protection features. Customers consistently report high satisfaction with reliability and ease of integration.
